events.title: ‘We are preserving a tradition’: how Ghana’s sensationalist film posters became collectible art
events.summary:
Hand-painted works are often wildly unfaithful to the movies they portray – reinterpretations that sometimes resulted in threats, insults and even physical attacks from viewers who felt duped

Sitting on his porch in Teshie near Accra, Heavy J dipped a brush into red oil paint and dabbed it carefully on to his canvas – a flour sack – adding blood to a knife being wielded by a man. Higher on the canvas, he had started on an outline of a skull.

Heavy J was creating a poster, but not as you might have expected for a horror film. Instead, it was for the animated fairytale The Little Mermaid. The man with the knife was not a killer but the film’s kind-hearted prince, Eric. The skull was also unrelated to the story. “We add more to make people interested,” said Heavy J, whose real name is Jeaurs Affutu.

The Neustrashimy suddenly announced it intended to fire its guns in the morning and requested that a British warship, which had been tracking it, retreat a couple of miles to a safe distance.

events.title: Stand still, make eye contact: Denmark tells tourists how to behave in a wolf encounter
events.summary:
Government urges visitors not to approach or feed wolves as it manages safety amid growing population

Danish authorities have advised tourists to stand still, make eye contact and put up “maximum resistance” in the event of attack if they encounter a wolf while on holiday.

After a 200-year absence, wolves started returning to Denmark in 2012. There are now estimated to be 49 wolves on the Danish peninsula of Jutland.

Continue reading...

events.title: Trump pledges to ‘take care of’ Houthis if group blocks Saudi ships and says US airlines can resume direct flights to Lebanon – live
events.summary:
The Iran-based group said it plans to impose an immediate maritime blockade of Saudi Arabia in the Red Sea

Lebanon’s president, Joseph Aoun, is due to meet Donald Trump for the first time today as he wraps up a four-day visit to Washington.

The meeting coincides with ongoing US-mediated talks between Lebanon and Israel. The two sides announced a “framework agreement” on 26 June which stipulates the withdrawal of Israeli forces from southern Lebanon as well as the disarmament of Hezbollah.

Continue reading...

events.title: Pete Hegseth faces questions over $87.6bn additional funding for Pentagon
events.summary:
Defense secretary and chair of the joint chiefs face Senate appropriations committee to continue Iran war effort

The US defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, walked into the Senate on Tuesday to defend the Trump administration’s $87.6bn supplemental funding request, facing lawmakers furious that the US-Israel war on Iran is costing far more than the White House has admitted.

Hegseth and the chair of the joint chiefs, Gen Dan Caine, faced the Senate appropriations committee, chaired by Susan Collins of Maine, as the Pentagon said it was running low on cash to keep the fight going. Of the total request, $67.1bn of the request is earmarked specifically to keep the war effort funded.

Continue reading...

events.title: Houthis threaten to attack shipping tankers if they use Saudi Arabian ports on Red Sea
events.summary:
Warning to shipping companies, on top of Iran’s chokehold on strait of Hormuz, may severely affect Saudi oil exports

  • Middle East crisis live – latest updates

Yemen’s Iranian-aligned Houthis have warned shipping companies that their tankers risk being attacked if they use Saudi Arabian ports, as the militia moved quickly to enforce a blockade announced the previous day.

The Houthi warning for the Red Sea, coming on top of Iran’s threat to shipping on the other side of the Arabian peninsular in the Gulf, could affect the substantial Saudi oil exports that have so far been unscathed by the months-long conflict and deepen the rapidly escalating crisis in the region.

Continue reading...

events.title: Zendaya faces backlash for wearing ancient Iranian jewellery at Odyssey event
events.summary:
Film star promotes Christopher Nolan film epic wearing gold artefacts dating back almost 3,000 years

The Odyssey is a story about a Greek king’s long journey home. But a pair of earrings worn on the new film adaptation’s press tour – and their long separation from their homeland – is sparking controversy.

At a photocall in London, Zendaya, who plays the goddess Athena in the Christopher Nolan film, wore a pair of earrings fashioned from Iranian artefacts dating back to 700BC.

Continue reading...

events.title: IDF accused of working with settlers after latest deadly West Bank raid
events.summary:
Residents of Deir Jarir say raids that killed two Palestinians late on Sunday are part of pattern of violence

Even by the brutal standards of militant settler violence in the West Bank, the raid on Deir Jarir late on Sunday night that killed two Palestinians and left another in hospital with a gunshot wound was extreme.

Palestinians in the town said settlers from a nearby outpost arrived after 9pm on Sunday claiming that they were searching for stolen livestock. Israeli soldiers and police soon joined them, protecting them as they sought to take several villagers’ sheep by force.

Continue reading...

events.title: India’s Cockroach movement protesters show no sign of backing down
events.summary:
Thousands of people again on the streets of Delhi calling for education reforms despite police violence on Monday

Taru had come to the protest prepared this time. In one hand was an anti-government poster and in the other was a bicycle helmet. “We need to have protection in case Delhi police beat us up again,” said the 25-year-old, who stood among her friends. “We are not scared of them and we won’t run away, no matter what they do. We’ll stay here till they listen.”

On Tuesday, central Delhi still thronged with thousands of protesters, as an unprecedented expression of dissent against the powerful ruling Narendra Modi government showed no sign of abating.

Continue reading...

events.title: Rescuers in Afghanistan search for missing and dead after flash floods destroy homes
events.summary:
National disaster agency confirms ‘severe financial and human losses’ in eastern province of Nuristan

Rescue crews in Afghanistan are searching for survivors and bodies among the rubble of houses destroyed by devastating flash floods that authorities said had killed nearly two dozen people and left more than 100 people missing.

The Afghanistan national disaster management authority said on Tuesday that 23 people had died, 80 had been injured and more than 100 were missing after floods struck the eastern province of Nuristan on Monday, causing an undetermined number of buildings to collapse. The provincial capital city of Parun was among the areas affected, authorities said.

Continue reading...

events.title: Tens of thousands of Cockroach movement protesters defy police with Delhi march
events.summary:
Youth-led group calls on education minister to quit in one of India’s largest anti-government rallies in years

Tens of thousands of protesters have defied police orders and descended on Delhi to demand the resignation of India’s education minister in one of the largest anti-government demonstrations in years.

A huge crowd, largely made up of students and gen Z-ers, filled the streets in the heart of India’s capital from early Monday morning shouting anti-government slogans. Hundreds had already camped out overnight.

Continue reading...

events.title: ‘When I get home, my whole body aches’: extreme heat saps health and wages of India’s poorest workers
events.summary:
Exclusive: Outdoor workers losing up to 9% of income as weather becomes ‘poverty multiplier’, research says

Extreme heat is pushing some of India’s poorest workers further into poverty, with income losses of up to 9% for outdoor workers, new research has found.

Analysis done by the International Institute for Environment and Development (IIED), and shared exclusively with the Guardian, found that extreme heat is acting as a “poverty multiplier” for hundreds of millions of workers in India’s informal sector.

Continue reading...

events.title: More than 500 feared dead after reports of two shipwrecks off Myanmar, UN says
events.summary:
Vessels believed to have departed from Myanmar in late June, with mostly Muslim Rohingya minority onboard

The United Nations has said more than 500 people are feared dead after reports of two large shipwrecks off Myanmar since late June.

The UN’s International Organization for Migration (IOM) and its refugee agency UNHCR voiced alarm in a joint statement at reports “that two boats carrying more than 500 people may have capsized off the coast of Myanmar in recent days”.

Continue reading...

events.title: Andy Burnham urges cabinet to examine ‘all possible ways’ to tackle cost of living crisis
events.summary:
As new prime minister briefs cabinet on ‘cost of living government’, critics ask where money will come from

Andy Burnham is under mounting pressure to explain how he will pay for his policy pledges as he implored his new cabinet to examine “all possible ways” to tackle the cost of living crisis.

On his first full day in power, the prime minister faced questions about how he would fund the removal of VAT from electricity bills and an expected uplift in defence spending – an issue that led his new chancellor, John Healey, to resign as Keir Starmer’s defence secretary last month.

Continue reading...

events.title: Anas Sarwar reportedly set to quit as Scottish Labour leader to be minister in Andy Burnham’s government – as it happened
events.summary:
Sarwar expected to be elevated to Lords to take up a ministerial post at revamped Department for Business, Innovation, Science and Trade

  • Burnham to cut VAT on electricity bills in first cost-of-living move

António Costa, the president of the European council, said in a post on X that he held a “good” phone call with Andy Burnham last night, in which he said they agreed to “further strengthen” EU-UK relations and continue to cooperate to support Ukraine.

He said there was also discussion about “common geopolitical challenges facing Europe”, which could be a reference to many things, including energy prices soaring due to the US-Israel war on Iran, Europe’s trading and security relationship with Washington, the future of Nato, the continent facing a wave of hybrid warfare, driven by Russia, or the surge of national populism and backlash against immigration.

Continue reading...

events.title: Former Met police chief admits surveillance of Stephen Lawrence’s family was institutionally racist
events.summary:
Paul Condon tells spycops inquiry force failed to properly control unit which committed ‘horrendous’ abuses

A former Metropolitan police commissioner has admitted that the surveillance of the family of Stephen Lawrence by undercover officers was institutionally racist, the spycops inquiry has heard.

While Paul Condon was in command of the Metropolitan police in the 1990s, a controversial undercover unit spied on the family as they sought to persuade Scotland Yard to properly investigate Lawrence’s racist murder.

Continue reading...

events.title: Fiscal hawk or big spender – what kind of chancellor will John Healey be?
events.summary:
Diligent, popular, unexpected pick for the Treasury has been given a straitjacket – and the job of funding PM’s policy promises

On the frequent occasions that John Healey would travel abroad as defence secretary, he was so determined to keep working he would get the crew to tether a desk to the inside of his military aircraft so he could read his papers.

His diligence is one reason why Andy Burnham, the new prime minister, made Healey his unexpected pick for chancellor on Monday. Allies say others include their shared commitment to re-industrialisation and devolution.

Continue reading...

events.title: Starmer allies see ‘hint of retribution’ in Burnham reshuffle
events.summary:
Former PM’s supporters question pledge to end factional infighting after more than half his cabinet removed or demoted

Allies of Keir Starmer have questioned Andy Burnham’s pledge to end factional infighting after he removed or demoted more than half of the outgoing prime minister’s cabinet in his first reshuffle.

“This was a deeply factional cabinet reshuffle,” one said. “The idea it was anything else is ridiculous.”

Continue reading...

events.title: Trial over Sacramento’s deadliest shooting ends in mistrial
events.summary:
Mtula Payton and Dandrae Martin were tried together over shooting on K Street in April 2022 that left six dead

The trial of two men accused of carrying out the deadliest shooting in the city of Sacramento, California, ended in a mistrial on Tuesday.

The suspects, Mtula Payton and Dandrae Martin, were tried together and faced up to 15 years to life in prison for their role in the shooting, which killed six people and injured at least a dozen. While they were not convicted for the deaths, they were found guilty of being in possession of firearms despite having prior felonies.

Continue reading...

events.title: Bay Area city manager charged for allegedly breaking into 66ft yacht while drinking
events.summary:
Sausalito city manager Elaine Forbes faces misdemeanor charges for allegedly trying to steal the three-story vessel

A Bay Area city manager will face misdemeanor charges for allegedly breaking into a 66ft yacht while drinking and trying to steal the vessel.

Elaine Forbes, Sausalito’s city manager, was arrested on the morning of 18 July after police received a call for a noise disturbance. Police say Forbes had “apparently broken into” a three-story yacht, where she was consuming alcohol.

Continue reading...

events.title: Senate committee advances nomination of Trump intelligence pick Jay Clayton
events.summary:
Committee voted 9-8 to advance Clayton’s nomination to serve as intelligence chief despite Democratic opposition

The Senate intelligence committee on Tuesday advanced the nomination of Jay Clayton to serve as the US intelligence director, despite criticism from Democrats.

Donald Trump had nominated Clayton, now the top federal prosecutor in the southern district of New York, for the role of the director of national intelligence (DNI) last month after the departure of Tulsi Gabbard.

Continue reading...

events.title: Bill Gates foundation met with Jeffrey Epstein about 30 times, external review finds
events.summary:
Staff ‘raised on multiple occasions’ risks of tech mogul’s philanthropic body associating with disgraced financier

A new external review commissioned by the Gates Foundation has found that there were “roughly 30 meetings” between Jeffrey Epstein and foundation leaders and staff – including Bill Gates – between 2011 and 2014, even as foundation staff “raised on multiple occasions the risks of associating with Epstein because of his prior conviction”.

The Gates foundation commissioned the review in March, following the justice department’s release earlier this year of millions of documents related to Epstein. The files included numerous references to Gates, several photographs of him, and records showing that he met with Epstein on multiple occasions, which prompted renewed scrutiny of Gates’s past ties to the disgraced financier.

Continue reading...

events.title: Trump to allow direct US flights to Lebanon after 41-year halt
events.summary:
Announcement comes after White House meeting with Lebanese president in which Trump vowed to help ‘a lot’

Donald Trump said on Tuesday he would allow American carriers to resume direct flights to Lebanon more than 40 years after the US suspended the route.

Direct flights between the US and Lebanon were suspended in 1985 by Ronald Reagan’s administration following the hijacking of TWA flight 847.

Continue reading...

events.summary: The movement, which began as a satirical protest, has evolved into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government.

events.summary: As Iranian attacks intensify, Jordan is emerging as the latest frontline state in the widening Middle East conflict. Why is the Hashemite Kingdom a target for Tehran?

events.summary: Turkey's main opposition CHP faces a potentially historic rupture after months of infighting over its leadership.

events.summary: After Spain won the FIFA World Cup, the Spanish Football Federation posted a photo of the trophy celebrations, raising questions about whether Donald Trump had been edited out. We took a closer look.

events.summary: The latest arrest comes two months after he was released from detention on charges including "insulting" President Recep Tayyip Erdogan.

events.title: French lawmakers approve social media ban for children
events.summary: Lawmakers in France gave the green light to a social media ban that could come into effect by the start of the new school year in September, becoming the first EU country to enact such a ban.

events.title: India: Police forcibly remove Rahul Gandhi from protest outside Modi residence
events.summary: Rahul Gandhi and other leaders of the main opposition Congress party have been dragged away by police from a rally outside PM Narendra Modi's residence, a day after dozens of youth protesters were arrested. Follow DW.

events.summary: German rapper Danger Dan and pianist Igor Levitt had been invited to play a song for the 100th episode of a political satire show on TV. The performance was canceled at the last minute due to its message.

events.title: Germany: Mass shooting suspect found dead in prison
events.summary: A man suspected of carrying out one of the worst mass shootings in recent German history was found dead in his cell after an apparent suicide.

events.title: China narrows AI gap, challenges US influence
events.summary: At the World Artificial Intelligence Conference in Shanghai, Beijing showcased advances in AI while promoting its vision of open-source development and global governance.

events.title: Is Germany investing in the right weapons?
events.summary: Germany wants to build up its army and pour hundreds of millions of euros into its defenses. But in a world where the battlefield is increasingly dominated by cheap drones, how many expensive tanks does the country need?

events.title: Ozempic maker sues US rival over ad campaign
events.summary: Novo Nordisk has claimed that Eli Lilly is using bad data to compare the companies' weight-loss and diabetes products in its ads.

events.summary: What started as a satirical protest against exam leaks in India has grown into a wider movement voicing young Indians' anger over jobs and education.

events.title: Italy: Protests in Bologna over death of immigrant in police custody
events.summary: Demonstrators are demanding answers over the death of 42-year-old Abderrahim Fakir. Local authorities have urged calm while the investigation is underway.

events.title: Why the youth jobs crisis is becoming global
events.summary: Young people in much of the world face growing competition for entry-level jobs as graduate numbers rise, hiring slows and AI-driven disruption adds uncertainty to career prospects.

events.title: China, Philippines clash in South China Sea ahead of ASEAN meeting
events.summary: China and the Philippines traded accusations after a violent altercation between navy personnel in the disputed South China Sea. The incident heightened tensions ahead of a major diplomatic summit in Manila.

events.title: How heat waves are messing with your sleep
events.summary: Residents across Europe have struggled to sleep during the latest record heat waves. But as the planet continues to warm, sleep deprivation could create an epidemic of mental and physical ill-health.

events.title: Germany news: Merz hosts Azerbaijan President Aliyev
events.summary: Talks between the German chancellor and Ilham Aliyev have focused on energy. Meanwhile, Interior Minister Alexander Dobrindt will present a report on sexual offenses against children. DW has more.

events.title: AliExpress hit with record EU fine
events.summary: The Chinese retailer has become the latest major online marketplace to face penalties under the EU’s Digital Services Act.

events.title: England and Liverpool football legend Kevin Keegan dies aged 75
events.summary: English former striker and manager Kevin Keegan has passed away at the age of 75 after a battle with cancer. He was a two-time European footballer of the year with Hamburg.

events.title: Russia says bus strike kills 5 in border region with Ukraine
events.summary: The strike comes as Ukraine launched more than hundreds of drones at Moscow after a Russian missile barrage on Kyiv, as President Zelenskyy sought to calm growing tensions over Ukraine’s military leadership.

events.title: UN chief Guterres to visit Cyprus in bid to revive peace talks
events.summary: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres will visit Cyprus next week in what is seen as an effort to kickstart the stalled peace process. Why now? What is known about the plan? And what might be different this time round?

events.title: Why a 90-year-old treaty limits NATO in the Black Sea
events.summary: A 90-year-old treaty still shapes the balance of power in the Black Sea. As Ukraine fights on against Russia, can NATO project power into waters it can't enter freely?

events.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's new prime minister
events.summary: Andy Burnham has been sworn in as the successor to British Prime Minister Keir Starmer. Burnham, the former Manchester mayor, has become a leading figure in the Labour Party and one of the UK's most popular politicians.

events.title: How 'blue dot fever' is affecting concerts
events.summary: Live music remains popular, but many concerts are struggling with unsold tickets and cancellations. While superstars fill stadiums, smaller venues and acts are facing a crisis.

events.title: Germany news: Resistance to Nazi terror commemorated
events.summary: Monday marks 82 years since "Operation Valkyrie," the failed attempt on Adolf Hitler's life in 1944. Meanwhile, Foreign Minister Wadephul is heading to Africa on official business.

events.title: Taking stock of 2026 World Cup: Football's biggest show yet
events.summary: A worthy world champion, a call from the US president, security concerns and a few pleasant surprises: The biggest World Cup ever is now over. So what will we remember about the 2026 World Cup?

events.title: Spain wins second World Cup after beating Argentina in final
events.summary: After a 16-year wait, Spain has claimed a second men's World Cup. Ferran Torres scored the winner in a deserved 1-0 victory over Argentina, which means Spain is now men's and women's world champions.

events.title: New Bundesliga TV deal in US prioritizes reach over money
events.summary: The Bundesliga has a new home on American screens after Germany's top football league left ESPN. The new deal is a sign of the times.

events.title: Spain: Emergency crews battle wildfire north of Madrid
events.summary: More than 25 firefighting planes have been deployed to battle the blaze in central Spain, which has been raging for three days.

events.title: EU ban on destroying unsold clothes takes effect
events.summary: Under new rules to reduce waste, large companies across the European Union can no longer dispose of unsold clothes, accessories and footwear.

events.title: China firm seeks damages over state control of British Steel
events.summary: The UK government took over British Steel last year to prevent the closure of the country's last primary steelmaking facility. China's Jingye Group, which bought the firm in 2020, claims it is owed compensation.

events.title: Russian army deserters in Germany: 'No choice but to run'
events.summary: Almost all asylum applications by Russian deserters are being denied by German authorities. DW and Russian-language media outlet Astra spoke to one of the young men who fears he'll be sent back to die on the front lines.

events.title: Russia's largest ballistic assault of war rocks Kyiv
events.summary: Fires broke out across the Ukrainian capital after Russia's latest missile attack, which used the highest number of ballistic missiles of the war so far. Several people have been injured in Kyiv.

events.title: Germany: CDU's Jens Spahn resigns amid surrogacy controversy
events.summary: Surrogacy is not permitted in Germany and the conservative Christian Democratic Union (CDU) opposes its legalization. Spahn and his husband had their child through a surrogate mother in the US.

events.title: Norway: Huge fire destroys 100 homes, sparks evacuations
events.summary: Firefighters from other areas of Norway were drafted in to help tackle the blaze in the southern city of Drammen.

events.title: Reproductive medicine laws in Germany 'shockingly unjust'
events.summary: Infertility affects one in six women in Germany. However, access to reproductive medicine is not available to all. Surrogacy as well as egg donation are banned.

events.title: Why did Jens Spahn's surrogacy spark debate in Germany?
events.summary: Jens Spahn, a key ally of Chancellor Friedrich Merz, is stepping down amid a controversy surrounding his decision to have a child via a surrogate mother. He had opposed legalizing surrogacy in Germany.

events.title: Germany cracks down on money laundering, tax fraud
events.summary: The German government plans to crack down on tax fraud, money laundering and illegally acquired assets with more audits and stiffer penalties.

events.title: Germany's 5,000 heat deaths: What the numbers reveal
events.summary: More than 5,100 people in Germany died from heat-related causes this year until the end of June, the Robert Koch Institute reports. But how can researchers prove that someone died because of soaring temperatures?

events.title: Merz and Macron: A Franco-German week of harmony
events.summary: Friedrich Merz and Emmanuel Macron did everything they could this week to demonstrate a close partnership. This is likely because both fear that the next French president could be nationalist Marine Le Pen.

events.title: Cities are getting smarter about water and other eco wins
events.summary: From flood-resistant infrastructure to new ways of securing drinking water, cities are adapting to the growing climate pressures of a hotter, drier world. Plus, tourists pitching in to help sea turtles.

events.title: How new PM Andy Burnham will change UK's international role
events.summary: From northwest England to the global stage: Andy Burnham becomes Britain's new prime minister on Monday. How will the former mayor of Manchester tackle international affairs?

events.title: The EU climate policy the world copied is under fire — what's at stake?
events.summary: The EU Commission has proposed to weaken Europe's emissions trading system, a global blueprint for pricing carbon and cutting CO2. How effective is the scheme and where does it fall short?

events.title: How AI and satellites help fight wildfires
events.summary: Wildfires are raging across Europe, Canada and beyond. To successfully fight these fires, speed is essential. A German startup uses satellite data and AI to spot fires early.

events.title: Iran's oil supply threat extends beyond Strait of Hormuz
events.summary: Oil and gas producers in the Gulf are seeking alternatives to the Strait of Hormuz as traffic again comes under fire. But is Iran also turning its attention to the pipelines meant to bypass the waterway?

events.title: German tenants need better heat protection
events.summary: Heat waves are becoming longer, hotter and more frequent. Record temperatures have left many tenants struggling. Now Chancellor Friedrich Merz has said the building law needs to be amended.

events.title: Monaco assassination plot takes a new turn
events.summary: A woman suspected of plotting to assassinate a Ukrainian-Cypriot businessman in Monaco has been found dead in Ukraine. Two men with ties to Ukraine's intelligence services have now been brought into custody.

events.title: Germany massively expands federal police powers
events.summary: The German government has increased the powers of its federal police, especially in the use of AI, surveillance and drone defenses. But experts warn of constitutional challenges.

events.summary: Germany's upper house of parliament, the Bundesrat, has introduced a bill seeking to ban the questioning of Israel's right to exist. It is now up to the lower house to decide whether to turn it into law.

events.title: US slaps 25% tariffs on Brazil with election looming
events.summary: The US will impose a 25% tariff on most imports from Brazil starting later this month. It's the first action in the Trump administration's new tariff strategy, after the Supreme Court struck down its prior impositions.

events.title: Ukraine lawmakers approve Koretskyi as new PM amid protests
events.summary: It is President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's second Cabinet shakeup in a year. He faces criticism for dismissing reformist Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

events.title: AI: Why Europe is falling behind, and how it can catch up
events.summary: Europe trails the US and China in artificial intelligence. Economist and Nobel laureate Philippe Aghion says better research funding, venture capital and AI innovation can help it catch up.

events.title: Will gold prices extend their record-breaking run?
events.summary: Predictions suggest the price of the precious metal will keep hitting new record highs as investors seek a safe bet in turbulent times. Some forecasts say prices could double within five years.

events.title: East-West border death zone now Germany's biodiversity oasis
events.summary: Many endangered species now live in the area that once formed the border between East and West Germany. Environment Minister Carsten Schneider recently visited the "Green Belt," and DW went along for the ride.

events.title: Could Russia be about to lose its grip on chess leadership?
events.summary: Russians have long pulled the strings when it comes to the governing body of international chess. But now two Germans are vying for FIDE's top job.

events.summary: Germany's chancellor has insisted that his coalition has "regained its footing" — despite dismal poll numbers. Friedrich Merz also cautioned against voting for the far-right AfD party.

events.title: No support for Jews fleeing Nazi Germany at Evian conference
events.summary: In July 1938, a conference was held in Evian, France, to address the issue of Jews being persecuted by the Nazis in Germany and Austria. Almost nobody in the world was willing to take them in as refugees.

events.summary: From the Falkland Islands to Diego Maradona and David Beckham, there is so much history between the two 2026 World Cup semifinalists.

events.title: Why Indonesia's economy is under pressure

events.title: Why is Zelenskyy reshuffling Ukraine's government?

events.title: 150 years of the Bayreuth Festival: Confronting a troubled legacy
events.summary: Should the past be confronted — or avoided? Why the Wagner festival's handling of its Nazi-era legacy remains a contentious issue in its 150th anniversary year.

events.title: How the 2016 coup attempt changed Turkey for good
events.summary: Ten years ago, parts of the Turkish military attempted to overthrow the government of President Recep Tayyip Erdogan. The consequences of the failed coup continue to shape Turkey to this day. DW looks back.

events.title: Germany's Merz receives death threat from Iranian newspaper
events.summary: In retaliation for Khamenei's death, a Tehran daily listed 13 world leaders, including German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, as targets. Politicians voiced concern.

events.title: T. rex 'Gus' sells for record-breaking $50.1 million
events.summary: The Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton surpassed its pre-sale estimate and set a new auction record for a dinosaur fossil.

events.title: Ukraine parliament accepts PM Svyrydenko's resignation
events.summary: With Ukraine "changing its political strategy," President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is trying to remake the government,

events.title: Micronutrients are good, a balanced diet is better
events.summary: Chronic illnesses, heart disease, obesity ― we know eating too much of the wrong foods can be unhealthy. But what about "good" foods: Is modern farming diluting micronutrients in our fruit and vegetables?

events.title: France arrests arson suspects amid Fontainebleau forest fire
events.summary: Firefighters are attempting to tackle a major fire burning since Sunday in the much-visited Fontainebleau forest, near France's capital Paris. Two people have been arrested for suspected arson.

events.title: Germany's Ahr Valley: Rebuilding better after deadly flood
events.summary: New walls, redesigned bridges and restored floodplains: Towns in a small German valley are testing how they can survive the next extreme flood.

events.title: Germany set to restrict its Freedom of Information Act
events.summary: Critics fear the reforms could weaken government transparency by curbing civil society's access to official information and limiting requests from foreigners.

events.title: World Cup: Balogun red and expansion keep FIFA in spotlight
events.summary: The fallout from Folarin Balogun's red card reversal continues as reports suggest the chairman of FIFA’s disciplinary committee made the decision unilaterally. Meanwhile, the World Cup could be set to expand once more.

events.title: Are German companies leaving the country?
events.summary: Should I stay or should I go? Given Germany's high cost and sluggish growth, some business leaders are mulling relocating, or at least investing abroad. DW has a look at the numbers.

events.title: California's largest lake is turning to dust
events.summary: The Salton Sea is drying up, worsening air pollution for nearby communities. Officials are racing to contain it, but for some families, it's already too late.

events.title: Germany's Ruhr Valley: Cities on brink of financial collapse
events.summary: With tax revenues down and social spending up, more and more cities and towns in the Ruhrgebiet are struggling. Oberhausen is particularly deep in the red.

events.title: Germany news: Neuschwanstein killer fights extradition to US
events.summary: A man who murdered a woman at Neuschwanstein Castle says he fears execution in the US. Germany has been hit by another heat wave, though no records are likely to be broken this time, forecasters say.

events.title: How a Jewish heir is taking on Germany's far-right AfD
events.summary: The AfD is using the iconic GDR-era Simson moped in its campaign. Now, a Jewish heir to the founding family is fighting to protect the company's legacy.

events.title: Heat is especially hard on people taking antidepressants
events.summary: High temperatures can increase the risk of dizziness, dehydration, exhaustion and other heat-related problems for people taking antidepressants.

events.title: Germany: Left-wing group says it is behind railway sabotage
events.summary: The "Angry Birds Commando" group said they placed incendiary devices on a railroad between the two busy cities of Cologne and Düsseldorf. The group blamed industrial technology for the destruction of the environment.

events.title: Germany's richest man takes on Big Tech
events.summary: Supermarket billionaire Dieter Schwarz is investing billions in his cloud company, Schwarz Digits, to rival Google, Microsoft and Amazon.

events.title: Mayor of Berlin says he won't run for reelection
events.summary: Berlin Mayor Kai Wegner withdrew from the reelection race this fall. His decision to play tennis during the power outage in January was among missteps that has dented his reputation.

events.title: Ukraine's Bidnyi 'outraged' by Russia's Olympic return
events.summary: In exclusive comments to DW, Ukraine's sports minister, Matviy Bidnyi, said he is outraged by the IOC opening the door to Russia's return to the Olympics

events.title: When London stank to high heaven
events.summary: In 1858, London sweltered through an unbearable heat wave. The River Thames reeked so badly it left people gasping for air. "The Great Stink" went down in history — and ultimately gave London a modern sewer system.

events.title: Vegan pregnancy tests and other eco wins this week
events.summary: Keeping cool in the heat with a millennia-old tradition in India, a German company making animal-free medical tests, and building from bottles in Mozambique.

events.title: Why the World Cup can be good for your mental health
events.summary: The World Cup can offer more than just entertainment, it can also benefit your mental well-being. DW explains why.

events.title: What's behind Germany's World Cup woes?
events.summary: Germany have endured three straight disappointing World Cups. Experts say the reasons why are connected to football, but also to German society.

events.title: What happens when your fun-loving grandfather — a good husband, an upright citizen — turns out to have been a Nazi?
events.summary: Since millions of NSDAP membership cards have been made available online, many Germans have been surprised to discover that their ancestors weren't always as innocent as family stories had led them to believe.

events.title: Bonnie Tyler, singer of 'Total Eclipse of the Heart,' dies at 75
events.summary: The Welsh singer was best known for the 1980s rock opera hit, along with other songs, including "It's a Heartache."

events.title: Is the Strait of Hormuz still Iran's trump card?
events.summary: Iran's latest attacks show it can easily disrupt shipping, energy markets and draw in Gulf neighbors. DW asks how much leverage Tehran really has over Washington and if its high-risk strategy could backfire.

events.title: Egypt's wrath after World Cup exit: 'The match was rigged'
events.summary: Egypt were left seething by their narrow and dramatic round-of-16 defeat against Argentina. Coach Hossam Hassan accused the referee of favoring the defending World Cup champions.

events.title: Assassin's Creed: Why pop culture is hooked to pirates
events.summary: Pirates have captured our imagination for centuries. With "Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced," gamers can now head off on another pirate adventure. How has our concept of pirates evolved with pop culture?

events.title: The Australian tree supercharging global wildfires
events.summary: As Europe experiences another summer of extreme heat and wildfire warnings, one tree imported from Australia is coming under renewed scrutiny across the world: eucalyptus.

events.title: Brain tumors: New vaccine offers patients hope for more time
events.summary: A new therapy offers people with seemingly incurable brain tumors scope for hope: It could slow the progression of the disease and extend patients' lives.

events.title: Conflict, climate change, cash-strapped: Why poverty persists
events.summary: With just under five years left to complete the UN's Sustainable Development Goals, a new report shows what's hampering progress. And what the solutions are.

events.title: Heat-resistant housing: How we need to adapt our homes
events.summary: In Germany, it is time to rethink and rebuild. Many apartments were not designed for heat waves. Fortunately, there are ways to adapt existing buildings to the changing climate — but air conditioning isn't the best.

events.title: Mbappe hits back at racist insults from Paraguay senator
events.summary: A Paraguayan lawmaker has sparked outrage with racist insults directed at footballer Kylian Mbappe. The French FA says it will take legal action, and President Emmanuel Macron has expressed support for the player.

events.title: Could football break away from FIFA and its World Cup?
events.summary: FIFA’s actions at the World Cup, and closeness to Donald Trump, have put the organization under fire. Federations, fans, coaches and players have gone public with complaints, but is there a way out?

events.title: Why Volkswagen's huge workforce became a costly burden
events.summary: The struggling German carmaker plans to cut up to 100,000 jobs from its 630,000-strong workforce. How did VW's headcount get so bloated, and will the cuts be enough to survive the Chinese EV onslaught?

events.title: World Cup 2026: Trump, FIFA and the Balogun controversy
events.summary: One of the few certainties of World Cup football has been rocked after Donald Trump lobbied FIFA to overturn a US red card suspension. The decision has caused outrage but it isn't completely without precedent.

events.title: Fashion against fascism: When trademarks deter neo-Nazis
events.summary: In Germany, where Nazi symbols are banned, activists are using trademark law to fight far-right merchandise.

events.title: Chinese Tianwen-2 space probe reaches asteroid for sampling
events.summary: After a yearlong flight of a billion kilometers, a Chinese space probe is set to take samples from an asteroid. The mission is part of what President Xi Jinping calls the country's "space dream."

events.title: European space telescope Euclid spots oldest quasars yet
events.summary: Think of quasars as time machines. Rare but revealing. They are light from the beginnings of the universe that let astronomers investigate how early galaxies formed.

events.title: Why US train travel lags behind Asia and Europe
events.summary: As flights get pricier and gas prices stay high, more US travelers are turning to trains — and hitting a system built for freight, not people.

events.title: Free electricity in Australia, and the week's other eco wins
events.summary: Germans save an ancient woodland from coal mining, Australia gives away surplus solar power, and how one city became the world's best place for cyclists?

events.title: Jürgen Klopp and Germany: A partnership long in the making
events.summary: In one of the worst-kept secret in football, the former Dortmund and Liverpool coach looks to be on the verge of becoming Germany's new boss.

events.title: Far-right AfD threatens eastern Germany's energy transition
events.summary: Germany's far-right AfD wants to revive coal and nuclear power and curb non-EU immigration. Businesses in eastern Germany warn that its policies could harm the economy.

events.title: Sick leave: Germany rising but not the worst in Europe
events.summary: Sick leave in Germany has hit a new record, with workers taking an average of nearly 20 days off per year. As Chancellor Merz cracks down, how does the country's absenteeism compare to other countries?

events.title: The downfall: Julian Nagelsmann out as Germany coach
events.summary: The German Football Associationand national team coach Nagelsmann have parted ways after Germany's poor display at the 2026 World Cup.

events.title: As heat waves loom, scientists wonder how humans will adapt
events.summary: Temperatures are set to soar in the US around the July 4 holiday, a week after Europe struck new records. With experts predicting more intense and more frequent heat waves in the future, can our bodies adapt?

events.title: Is Mexico the 2026 World Cup's best host country?
events.summary: Mexico has fully embraced its role as a World Cup host. Visitors are raving about the atmosphere and say they wish that there were more knockout-stage matches in Mexico as opposed to in the United States.

events.title: Fact check: Racist German fan petition story false
events.summary: It has been claimed that following Germany's World Cup exit, some fans launched a petition seeking to ban "African" and "Muslim" players from the national team — and that DW reported the story. This is not true.

events.title: US declines to renew USMCA trade pact with Mexico, Canada
events.summary: Lengthy negotiations are expected as the Trump administration seeks changes to a trade deal with Mexico and Canada that has streamlined supply chains across North America.

events.title: Bundesliga: Kathleen Krüger takes over as Hamburg's head of sport
events.summary: Hamburg have presented Kathleen Krüger as the club's new board member responsible for sport. She is the first woman to fill such a role at a Bundesliga club on a permanent basis.

events.title: Hydration breaks: The most controversial issue at the World Cup
events.summary: The mandatory hydration breaks at the 2026 World Cup have sparked debate in the stands, on the pitch, and far beyond.

events.title: Digital euro: The plan to Trump-proof the EU's economies
events.summary: The EU’s central bank is preparing a digital euro to cut dependence on Visa, Mastercard and Apple Pay. Success hinges on giving consumers an easy-to-use payment system without prompting a flight from bank savings.

events.title: Germany saves forest from mining, US opens land to drilling
events.summary: Germans have won a fight to keep a coal mine out of an ancient forest. In the US, the Trump administration is opening protected lands for drilling and development.

events.title: Portugal's 'golden visa' delays spark legal fight
events.summary: After waiting years for their "golden visas," investors are taking Portugal to court. Tougher citizenship rules have added fuel to the growing dispute.

events.title: Art biennial Manifesta revives Germany's postwar churches
events.summary: Following the destruction of World War II, around 1,000 new churches were built in Germany's Ruhr region. Today, many stand empty, but the contemporary art show Manifesta is giving them a new lease on life.

events.title: Comedy legend Mel Brooks turns 100
events.summary: A parody of Adolf Hitler in the landmark 1968 movie "The Producers" was just the beginning for US actor Mel Brooks. The 100-year-old comedian is still on a roll today.

events.title: Creative climate messaging and other eco wins of the week
events.summary: An artist in Mali and climate scientists in the US are finding new ways to inform the public about the climate crisis.

events.title: Wagner festival reinstates Holocaust memorial event after cancellation
events.summary: The Bayreuth Festival had canceled a planned memorial lecture featuring Jewish author and broadcaster Michel Friedman, drawing sharp criticism.

events.title: Bayer: Why so many Roundup weedkiller lawsuits?
events.summary: Bayer was sued for failing to add cancer warnings to its controversial pesticide Roundup when US regulators opposed adding one. DW asks if the latest Supreme Court win will shield the chemical giant from paying billions.

events.title: Here's what southern Europeans know about surviving heat waves
events.summary: People in countries like Greece, Italy and Spain have long lived with high temperatures. Here's what more northerly countries can learn from them about managing heat waves as they become more common.

events.title: Human rights expert Philippe Sands wins German Peace Prize
events.summary: From advocating for the law of ecocide to representing Palestinian rights, the descendant of Holocaust survivors tirelessly fights against genocide and crimes against humanity.

events.title: How countries live with earthquakes and build resilience
events.summary: Earthquakes are a fact of nature, but living with earthquakes doesn't have to mean living in fear. DW takes a closer look at some countries showing how preparation, innovation and determination can save lives.

events.title: Why Germans don't have air conditioning
events.summary: Unlike the US or parts of Asia, many homes in Germany and northern Europe aren't equipped to deal with extreme heat. But as the world gets hotter, trends are changing.

events.title: Heat wave: Why you should skip that cold beer or cocktail
events.summary: Summer, the FIFA World Cup and a cold beer. Sounds like a dream? Be careful, though; the combination of heat and alcohol can quickly take a very bad turn.

events.title: European heat wave is not normal summer weather
events.summary: Human-induced climate change has intensified temperatures, making this year's record heat wave up to 4 degrees Celsius hotter.

events.title: Trump offers Iran an economic lifeline — with strings
events.summary: Iran has seen a speedy rebound in oil exports after the US-Iran deal granted immediate waivers on US sanctions. Relief on frozen assets and a $300 billion reconstruction fund now hinge on Tehran meeting key conditions.

events.title: Why Austrian author Ingeborg Bachmann remains a literary icon
events.summary: A new documentary featuring Sandra Hüller celebrates the Austrian poet and author who was a literary star of her era. She would have turned 100 this year.

events.title: Experts axed by Trump keep climate information flowing
events.summary: Fired US federal workers have revived a defunct climate website — pushing back as the Trump administration escalates cuts to publicly funded science and research.

events.title: Europe is spending billions to rearm, so why the delays?
events.summary: European countries have been trying to scale up their military capabilities, but their current defense production and procurement model is not fit for purpose, say experts.

events.title: More than a unibrow: The enduring appeal of Frida Kahlo
events.summary: She shunned feminine beauty ideals, was bisexual and a political radical, all while being brutally honest in her art. A major Frida Kahlo exhibition opens at London's Tate Modern.

events.title: 75 years of Germany's Goethe-Institut
events.summary: For 75 years, the Goethe-Institut has stood for cultural exchange, education and partnerships around the globe — even under challenging political conditions.

events.title: The man who knew, but didn't say: Alan Greenspan (1926-2026)
events.summary: Alan Greenspan’s reputation as head of the US Federal Reserve was legendary — not least because of his reputation for convoluted statements. However, the 2008/9 financial crisis took a toll on his standing.

events.title: How TikTok-style videos keep human brains hooked on content
events.summary: TikTok, Instagram and YouTube clips are designed to overwhelm the brain's pleasure circuitry and keep people watching. Researchers say short-form videos may only be the start when it comes to harvesting human attention.

events.title: Zelensky fires army chief Oleksandr Syrsky, ‌a 'victory for protestors'
events.summary: President ​Volodymyr ​Zelensky said on Tuesday that Major ​General ‌Mykhailo ⁠Drapaty ‌will take over as Ukraine's ⁠commander-in-chief after firing Soviet-trained top commander Oleksandr Syrsky. "This is a victory for the protestors who took to the streets a week ago, when Zelensky fired Fedorov," says FRANCE 24 correspondent Gulliver Cragg. "Their main demand was that Syrsky be fired." He added that "Drapaty was the one Fedorov wanted to see in that position to replace Syrsky".

events.title: Senegal u-turns to back ex-president Sall to lead UN
events.summary: In tonight's edition, a deadly ambush in northern Mali leaves at least 50 soldiers dead. Also, Nigeria is facing a new challenge in its own war against jihadists as foreign fighters are joining insurgent groups. And Senegalese President Bassirou Diomaye Faye is now backing former president Macky Sall's bid for UN Secretary-General.

events.title: Ukraine's Zelensky fires military chief after protests and names a replacement
events.summary: President Volodymyr Zelensky on Tuesday fired Gen. Oleksandr Syrsky as commander-in-chief of Ukraine’s armed forces, following days of mass protests in Kyiv and other cities nationwide demanding his removal. Zelenskyy appointed Mykhailo Drapatyi as the new army chief, according to a statement on social media.

events.title: 🔴 Zelensky fires Ukraine army chief Oleksandr Syrsky
Zelensky sacks Ukraine army chief Syrsky after protests over rival minister's dismissal
events.summary: President Volodymyr Zelensky on Tuesday dismissed Ukraine's military head Oleksandr Syrsky following days of public pressure and named Major ​General ‌Mykhailo ⁠Drapaty, an ally of ousted defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov, as the new commander-in-chief.

events.title: Joseph Aoun in Washington: Bilal Saab firmly believes 'this meeting couldn't have gone any better'
events.summary: François Picard is pleased to welcome Bilal Saab, Senior Managing Director of the Trends Research Group and a close friend of Lebanese President Joseph Aoun. Mr. Saab offers a cautiously optimistic assessment of a pivotal moment in Lebanese diplomacy, arguing that President Aoun's visit to Washington represents an important opportunity to consolidate international backing for Lebanon's sovereignty and state institutions. While acknowledging the formidable structural challenges posed by Hezbollah, regional tensions, and extremely complex Israeli-Lebanese security challenges, Mr. Saab contends that success will not come from military coercion, but rather a focus on the gradual strengthening of the Lebanese state. According to Mr. Saab, Lebanon is both a testing ground for American regional diplomacy and a fragile experiment in state-building that will require sustained political support and not symbolic declarations.

events.title: Zelensky faces deepening political crisis as protests dig in
events.summary: In Ukraine, protesters say they’re giving Volodymyr Zelensky until Friday to re-instate the popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, and fire General Oleksandr Syrsky, the military chief of staff. The President removed Fedorov from his post last week, citing a clash between the two men. The protests have brought Syrsky’s unpopularity to the forefront. Through credited with stopping the Russian invaders at the gates of Kyiv in 2022, he’s since gained a reputation for brutality, and for wasting lives unnecessarily. He's also, as our correspondent Gulliver Cragg reports, accused of tolerance for corruption.

events.title: Lebanon's president urges Trump to 'keep supporting' its army
events.summary: US President Donald Trump vowed Tuesday to help Lebanon "a lot" as its president, Joseph Aoun, sought Washington's backing for a fragile effort to disarm Hezbollah and forge a path to peace with Israel. Reporting from Jerusalem, FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky explains how the meeting between Aoun and Trump reprents "a slap in the face" for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

events.title: French parliament backs social media ban for under-15s in EU first
events.summary: FRANCE 24's Clovis Casali reports from France's National Assembly, where lawmakers have approved a ban on social media for children and teenagers under-15, the first EU country to do so. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Philippe Coen, lawyer and founder of the NGO Respect Zone.

events.title: Outrage in France as Macron appoints senator who fought gay marriage to key rights job
events.summary: French lawmakers on Tuesday narrowly approved President Emmanuel Macron’s candidate to serve as Défenseur des droits (human rights ombdusman), despite vehement opposition from groups arguing that Senator François-Noël Buffet’s views run counter to the mission of defending the rights of the most vulnerable.

events.title: Evenepoel beats Pogacar by seconds in stage 16 of Tour de France
events.summary: Olympic champion Remco Evenepoel powered to victory in the Tour de France's stage 16 time trial on Tuesday, beating overall leader Tadej Pogacar by 28 seconds to trim the Slovenian's advantage.

events.title: As he hosts the Lebanese president, Trump says the US 'will help' the country
events.summary: US and Lebanese Presidents Donald Trump and Joseph Aoun met for the first time ever on July 20, as Trump hosted his Lebanese counterpart in the White House. This comes as the Lebanese army is taking over pilot safe zones in South Lebanon as pressure builds on the government to disarm Hezbollah and it seeks to get Israel to leave the region. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson tells us what to know from that meeting.

events.title: Replay: Lebanon's Joseph Aoun and US's Donald Trump hold press conference
events.summary: Rewatch as Lebanese President Joseph Aoun meets US President Donald Trump as pressure mounts on Beirut to disarm Hezbollah.

events.title: 'We've got to be prepared to reprioritise': UK's PM holds first cabinet meeting
events.summary: Andy Burnham told the inaugural meeting of his cabinet on July 21 that he would lead a "cost-of-living government", after unveiling an energy tax cut on his first full day as prime minister. At the same time, Burnham called for 'fiscal discipline', just as critics are doubting the VAT cut can be funded by the scrapping of former prime minister Keir Starmer's digital ID scheme. FRANCE 24's Rochelle Ferguson-Bouyahi takes a look.

events.title: 'It has to work': France nears approval of under-15 social media ban
events.summary: France's two houses of parliament are expected to give final approval to legislation that bars under-15s from social media. The bill comes amid concern over mental health of young people. If approved, France would follow Australia's lead, where the world's first ban ​for under-16s ‌on platforms including Facebook, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube came into ⁠force in December.

events.title: Beirut hopes to gain Trump support to bring 'normality' back to south Lebanon
events.summary: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun is set to meet with his US counterpart Donald Trump in Washington on July 21, as Beirut's military moved into an area of the south under a deal with Israel to disarm Hezbollah. The pilot 'safe zones'  comses as Lebanon is under pressure to prove that it can and will disarm Iran-backed Hezbollah, which Israel has set as a precondition for ending its occupation in southern Lebanon. FRANCE 24's Renée Davis tells us more from Beirut.

events.title: India's Cockroach protests vow to continue despite police crackdown
events.summary: The leaders of India's youth-led Cockroach movement are vowing to continue protesting a day after police cracked down on demonstrators. They have been demanding the country's education minister, Dharmendra Pradhan, resign amid repeated leaks for entrance tests to medical schools. While two of the movement's members met with a government minister on July 20th, India's prime minister, Narendra Modi, is failing to address the issue for now. Delano D'Souza reports.

events.title: Microsoft strikes 'multibillion-dollar' deal with French AI firm Mistral
events.summary: Microsoft and French startup Mistral said Tuesday they had signed a "multibillion-dollar" deal for the US tech giant to use the AI developer's computing infrastructure in Europe.

events.title: Why France's D-Day landing beaches could join Unesco's heritage site list
events.summary: Normandy's D-Day landing beaches could soon be listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site -- marking the culmination of a project launched nearly 20 years ago. The designation would recognise "outstanding universal value" and raise the international profile of the beaches -- including one where Allied forces built an artificial harbour. The UN's cultural agency is set to announce its decision between July 24 and 27.

events.title: ‘I hear you’: Burnham’s pledge to ‘knock money off bills immediately’ signals politics of deliverism
events.summary: Stuart Norval is pleased to welcome Matthew Torbitt, former Labour Party advisor, political commentator, and anti-homelessness campaigner. With only weeks to prepare for a long-term 10-year programme for government, Burnham appears to be prioritising measures that demonstrate immediate responsiveness to economic insecurity. For Torbitt, this reflects a politics of “deliverism” rooted in Burnham’s ability to communicate empathy and urgency. His appeal rests partly on an image of ordinariness, relatability and trustworthiness: a PM who appears to understand everyday hardship and who can translate that understanding into tangible action.

events.title: 'France wants to lead the way' with under-15s social media ban
events.summary: French lawmakers are expected to pass a bill on July 21st banning social media access to children under 15. If approved, France would follow Australia's lead, where the world's first ban ​for under-16s ‌on platforms including Facebook, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube came into ⁠force in December, as concerns grow over the impact social media has on the health and safety of minors.

events.title: What the UK's new government says about the country's political turn
events.summary: Andy Burnham gathered his new cabinet on July 21 as he got down to work on his first full day as Britain's prime minister. This comes after he unveiled an energy tax cut aimed at easing cost-of-living pressures. The new government gathers many fresh faces as Burnham ousted most of Starmer's supporters, signalling a new left-ward direction for the government. FRANCE 24's Rochelle Ferguson-Bouyahi tells us more.

events.title: Israel and Lebanon trial 'safe zones' in southern Lebanon as part of deal
events.summary: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun is in Washington to meet with US President Donald Trump in the first official visit by a Lebanese head of state since 2009. It comes at a moment of high stakes for the country. Israeli forces are now occupying a significant portion of southern Lebanon following rocket attacks by Hezbollah on northern Israel. Tel Aviv insists that the Iran-backed militia be disarmed before it withdraws, something the Lebanese army is likely unable to accomplish. As a result, Israel has launched a '"pilot programme'" for "safe zones", which effectively sees Israel hand control of three Lebanese villages to the Lebanese Army, as Beirut tries to assert its authority.

events.title: Ukraine plunges into political crisis as anger over defence minister's dismissal grows
events.summary: Street protests were held for a fifth day on July 19 in Ukraine, over the firing of Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov. Fedorov was dismissed following frequent clashes with top Army general Oleksandr Syrsky, sparking indignation particularly among young Ukrainians, who credit the tech-savvy 35-year old Fedorov with innovative drone tactics that have given Ukraine new battlefield momentum against the Russian invasion. Fedorov has publicly blamed the 60-year-old Syrsky for his ouster. President Volodymyr Zelensky is now seeking to defuse the mounting political crisis and restore unity to the country's military.

events.title: France approves blanket social media ban for under-15s
events.summary: France has approved a blanket ban on under-15 children using social media, the first European country to introduce such a restriction.

events.title: New defence secretary hits out at Russia after live-fire exercise 40 miles off UK coast
events.summary: New defence secretary Wes Streeting has criticised a live-fire military exercise carried out by a Russian warship around 40 nautical miles (46 miles) off the British coast, calling it "performative and irresponsible".

events.title: At least 10 dead in India tunnel disaster with 17 feared trapped
events.summary: At least 10 people have died in a disaster inside an under-construction tunnel in India.

events.title: Woman seriously injured in suspected stabbing at church
events.summary: A woman in her 50s has been seriously injured in a suspected stabbing at a church in Ireland.

events.title: Father, mother, their three daughters and son killed in Israeli airstrike in Gaza
events.summary: A family of six have been killed in an Israeli airstrike in Gaza.

events.title: Parents urged to stop buying  e-scooters as 'toys' as Ireland brings in new age limit
events.summary: Ireland's deputy prime minister has urged parents to stop buying electric scooters as "toys" for their children ahead of new age limits coming into force.

events.title: Why last two years are worrying for future of Arctic
events.summary: Arctic winter sea ice is back in rapid decline after a slowdown in melting at the start of the decade, scientists have found.

events.title: 'One in 15 million chance' identical quadruplets born in Australia
events.summary: An Australian woman has given birth to identical quadruplets conceived naturally from a single embryo - in a one in 15 million chance occurrence.

events.title: Rare Titanic letter written days before sinking to go on sale
events.summary: A rare Titanic letter written by a passenger days before he died in the disaster is expected to sell for up to £35,000 at auction.

events.title: Police clash with protesters in Italy after man dies during arrest
events.summary: Protesters have clashed with police in Italy after the death of a Moroccan man after officers restrained him on the ground.

events.title: Record fish kill reported in Sint Maarten as crews struggle to remove carcasses
events.summary: Thousands of dead fish have blanketed a canal in Sint Maarten

events.title: Two of Puerto Rico's top government officials to step down as corruption allegations persist
events.summary: Puerto Rico’s governor says that her embattled chief of staff, Francisco Domenech, and deputy chief of staff, Itza García, are stepping down next month as they deny corruption allegations

events.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy fires military chief after protests and names a replacement
events.summary: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has fired Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi as commander-in-chief of Ukraine’s armed forces

events.title: Lawyer in sensitive Vatican abuse case issues scathing critique of church's secretive legal system
events.summary: The lawyer for five women who say they were sexually, psychologically and spiritually abused by a once-famous Jesuit priest has penned a scathing denunciation of the Vatican’s secretive legal system

events.summary: A federal appeals court has rejected a request by Joe Biden to block the release of audio recordings and transcripts of his interviews with a memoir ghostwriter before he was elected president.

events.title: Sen. Darline Graham says she’s running for a full 6-year term after Trump urged her to jump into the race
events.summary: President Donald Trump said he had given his “complete and total endorsement” to Graham, who was appointed to finish her late brother’s Senate term.

events.title: 6,600 noncitizens were registered to vote in N.J. because of a ‘software error,’ governor says
events.summary: Democratic Gov. Mikie Sherrill said a review found that around 400 noncitizens cast ballots in elections from 2023 to 2024.

events.title: Father, son charged with kidnapping two U.S. Forest Service biologists at gunpoint
events.summary: Joseph and Phoenix Henrichsen were charged with kidnapping a federal employee. “I’ve taken two fed biologists hostage,” the father is accused of saying in a voicemail.

events.title: FDA says it’s still investigating Taylor Farms in cyclosporiasis outbreak
events.summary: Taylor Farms continues to be the subject of a Food and Drug Administration investigation into the growing cyclosporiasis outbreak, despite a false positive test result, the agency said Monday.

events.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy fires his commander in chief after protests and major rift
events.summary: The firing caps a major crisis for Volodymyr Zelenskyy after Ukrainians took to the streets to call for Oleksandr Syrskyi’s resignation.

events.title: Graphic: Tracking ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz
events.summary: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has been at a near-standstill for weeks amid the Iran war, sending prices of oil and other key goods soaring.

events.title: Trump vows revenge on Iran and what to watch in Arizona’s primaries: Morning Rundown
events.summary: Trump enters an uncertain moment in an Iran war threatening to become a quagmire.

events.title: Two American tourists hospitalized in stabbing attack near Greece’s Acropolis
events.summary: Greek police say that a man stabbed two American tourists in front of the entrance of the Acropolis in Athens, sending the couple to the hospital with injuries. NBC News’ Claudio Lavanga reports the man was arrested after police intervened.

events.title: President Trump vows revenge on Iran for killing U.S. service members
events.summary: After a weekend of deadly attacks, the Pentagon has released the identities of some of the fallen service members, including 1st Lt. Tyler James Feehan from Hawaii and Pvt. Isabella Gonzales from Texas, who were killed from an Iranian attack. NBC’s Keir Simmons reports from the region.

events.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on dairy, alcohol, and cars from Canada
events.summary: The Trump administration imposed 50% tariffs on three categories of imports from Canada, dairy, alcohol, and cars, citing discriminatory actions. NBC News' Monica Alba reports on how Trump is also considering additional tariffs due to the Canadian wildfires.

events.title: Tate brothers in Miami court after new U.K. charges
events.summary: Andrew and Tristan Tate appeared in a Miami courtroom after  British prosecutors unveiled a wave of new charges, including rape. Authorities from the U.K. are seeking to extradite the Tate brothers. NBC News' Danny Cevallos reports on the 38 charges they face.

events.title: U.S. military identifies soldiers killed in Iranian attack in Jordan
events.summary: The Pentagon identified the soldiers as 25-year-old Tyler James Feehan of Hawaii and 19-year-old Isabella Gonzales of Texas.

events.title: Andy Burnham becomes seventh British prime minister in a decade
events.summary: The new Labour Party leader, Andy Burnham, replaced Keir Starmer as British prime minister, inheriting a country beset by low wages and internal strife.

events.title: Graphic: The U.K. is in a new era of leadership turnover
events.summary: The U.K. has had seven prime ministers since 2016, compared with just three from 2000 to 2015.

events.title: Tate brothers arrested by U.S. authorities, face additional rape charges in the U.K.
events.summary: Social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate were taken into custody by federal law enforcement officials in Miami on Saturday, the U.S.

events.title: U.S. and Iran trade strikes after two U.S. service members killed in Jordan
events.summary: Two U.S. service members were killed Friday while defending against an Iranian ballistic missile and drone attack in Jordan, and another service member is missing, U.S.

events.title: Iran and Kuwait report attacks on water and power infrastructure as strikes escalate
events.summary: Critical water and power infrastructure sites have been damaged as the U.S. and Iran continue to trade strikes, while the parched Middle East reels from temperatures of 100 degrees and beyond.

events.title: U.S. completes latest round of strikes on Iran’s military sites as Hormuz tensions climb
events.summary: The United States attacked bridges and other key infrastructure in southern Iran late Friday, capping nearly a week of strikes aimed at intensifying pressure on Tehran to give up control of the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Pro-Kremlin blogger who turned on Putin is arrested as Russia steps up crackdown
events.summary: A former loyalist blogger turned fierce critic of Russian President Vladimir Putin has been detained and accused of spreading false information about the armed forces, state media reported Friday.

events.title: American woman held in Iran since 2024 released, Trump announces
events.summary: Jared Genser, a lawyer who has been involved with high-profile cases of U.S. citizens detained in Iran, named the released citizen as his client, Dena Karari.
